primary_accounting_issue: Déterminer si le revenu provenant d’une licence de propriété intellectuelle est comptabilisé à un moment précis ou sur la durée du droit, selon la nature de la promesse de licence en IFRS 15.

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I selected IFRS 15 because it is the only candidate governing standard in the provided context, and under the stated precedence rule no overlapping lower-precedence document exists to displace it.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: low: the context includes explicit licence-specific guidance in IFRS 15 that directly addresses the timing of revenue recognition for intellectual property licences.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern how to determine the nature of a licence promise and whether revenue from a licence is recognised over time or at a point in time.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs provide the core transfer-of-control framework and the over-time versus point-in-time recognition bases that the licence guidance applies.
These paragraphs support the measurement of progress when a licence-related performance obligation is satisfied over time.
These paragraphs modify the revenue recognition pattern for sales-based or usage-based royalties related to licences of intellectual property.

peripheral_authority.reason: These paragraphs address over-time recognition for created assets and enforceable rights to payment, which are not the licence-specific peer models for this issue.
These paragraphs concern transaction price and variable consideration generally rather than identifying the core peer timing models for licence revenue.
These paragraphs relate to significant financing components, which do not determine whether licence revenue is recognised over time or at a point in time.
These paragraphs address non-cash consideration and consideration payable to a customer, not the top-level licence timing models.
These paragraphs address principal-versus-agent assessment, which is topically related to revenue but not relevant to identifying licence timing approaches.

treatment_families.family: Distinct licence of intellectual property: determine whether the promise is to provide access to intellectual property or to provide use of intellectual property
Licence not distinct from other promised goods or services: assess the combined performance obligation under the general transfer-of-control model
Sales-based or usage-based royalty on a licence of intellectual property: special royalty recognition constraint

approaches.label: Right to access intellectual property
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 15 treats this as a distinct licence model, with revenue recognised over time when the criteria in paragraph B58 are met.

approaches.label: Right to use intellectual property
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 15 treats this as a distinct licence model, with revenue recognised at a point in time when the criteria in paragraph B58 are not met.

approaches.label: Revenue recognised over time
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Where a licence is not distinct and is part of a combined performance obligation, IFRS 15 requires assessment under the general over-time recognition model.

approaches.label: Revenue recognised at a point in time
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Where a licence is not distinct and the combined performance obligation does not meet over-time criteria, IFRS 15 requires point-in-time recognition.

approaches.label: Sales-based or usage-based royalty recognition
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 15 provides a distinct recognition model for royalties promised in exchange for a licence of intellectual property.
